title: Finite K Nearest Neighbors

solver_reuse.target: finite nearest-neighbor bad squared-distance QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact finite replay computes the squared Euclidean distance 18 from the first query point to a fixed training point, while a malformed row claims 16, isolating a compact scalar Farkas conflict beneath the neighbor ranking.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional nearest-neighbor rows only when they add distinct tie-breaking, weighted-vote, alternative-metric, dimensionality, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep consistency, Bayes-risk, curse-of-dimensionality, and floating-point distance claims in theorem or statistical-inference lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed finite training table, rational query points, exact squared Euclidean distances, k-nearest neighbor selection with a strict rank gap, and majority-vote class counts as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false squared-distance claim through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting distance equation.
Expose the pack through statistics, probability, discrete/counting, nearest-neighbor, exact-vs-floating, and learner-query surfaces.

Keep nearest-neighbor consistency, Bayes-risk bounds, curse-of-dimensionality behavior, metric and weighting policy, tie-breaking policy, continuous feature spaces, and floating-point distance computation marked theorem, statistical-inference, or numerical-honesty horizon.
